claim: Cantonese has a bare comparative with a gradable predicate followed by optional standard and (jat1) di1
exact_support: Section 7 examples (30)-(31): Mary gou1 (Peter) (jat1) di1; standard optional
limitations: Single-author formal analysis; lexical productivity and discourse distribution not experimentally tested
parser_implication: Create a research subtype distinct from gwo3 comparison

claim: (jat1) di1 is analyzed as a differential measure phrase rather than the comparative functional marker
exact_support: Section 7 states di1 represents the measure phrase/how much taller and must follow an overt standard
parser_implication: Preserve overt differential and do not equate di1 with gwo3

claim: An overt standard precedes di1; standard after di1 is rejected in the source judgment
exact_support: Example (31) marks Mary gou1 di1 Peter as unacceptable
limitations: Judgment scope and speaker sample are not reported
parser_implication: Treat standard order as a strong boundary question, not a universal executable rule yet

claim: The gwo3 comparative requires an overt standard under the source analysis, unlike the bare di1 comparative
limitations: Other analyses or discourse ellipsis may differ
parser_implication: Keep UC-RQ-026 separate from UC-RQ-005 gwo3 comparative

claim: Adjectival di1 is treated as a bound comparative suffix
exact_support: Paper examples 靚-啲 pretty+er and Table 1 遲-啲 late-Comp later
limitations: Segmentation study does not establish full syntactic scope
parser_implication: Distinguish suffixal di1 from nominal di1

claim: An A-di1 form can function adverbially
exact_support: Section 4.6 states 遲-啲 later can also act as an adverbial
limitations: One example does not establish all temporal or manner subclasses
parser_implication: Do not force every A-di1 into adjectival predication

claim: The segmentation scheme was applied to 150,000 HKCanCor characters with high agreement
exact_support: Method and reported mean pairwise agreement 97.5%
limitations: Agreement concerns segmentation, not naturalness or construction productivity

claim: Suffixal di1 can mean a little bit, a bit, or a bit more and occurs after adjectives in comparisons
exact_support: Entry examples 細啲, 靚啲, 好啲喇, 講慢啲, 拉過啲張枱
limitations: Dictionary senses combine syntactic environments and are not a controlled grammar study
parser_implication: Use examples to motivate contextual subtypes, not unrestricted matching

claim: Post-predicate di1 occurs in property and postverbal action contexts
exact_support: Project source matrix records 貴係貴啲 and 行快啲
limitations: Pedagogical source does not establish full productivity
parser_implication: Preserve property versus action order and context

claim: 講慢啲 is attested in a polite directive
exact_support: Project source matrix records 唔該講慢啲
limitations: One teaching example cannot establish universal V+A+di1 syntax
parser_implication: Retain exact postverbal adjustment profile only

claim: 快啲 can precede a directional VP in a directive
exact_support: Project source matrix records 快啲行上去啦
limitations: Does not prove free alternation with 行快啲

claim: Cantonese adjective comparison contains multiple distinct strategies
exact_support: Chapter 9 separately lists change-of-state, equative, negative/interrogative, excessive, and superlative profiles
limitations: Page does not directly map the complete post-predicate di1 family
parser_implication: Keep UC-RQ-026 bounded within broader comparison ontology

claim: Runtime has broad DegreeMannerAdverbial and pre/postverbal wrappers for X+di1
exact_support: Probes 高啲, 快啲嚟, 講慢啲, 行快啲
limitations: Internal behavior is not linguistic evidence and contains scope errors
